content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 4.5.1–4.5.3 WARN — push entitlement (DealAlerts.entitlements:aps-environment) paired with a fixed hourly repeating notification (NotificationScheduler.swift:9 `timeInterval: 3600, repeats: true`) and promotional metadata ("alert you EVERY HOUR … unlimited notifications, all day long", description.txt:3) may trigger review questions about promotional push spam.
Pierre: Apple's 4.5.4 guidance requires push to be user-controlled and not used for spam or promotions without consent. Here both the code and store description promise automatic hourly promotional alerts unrelated to a user-initiated event, which reviewers may read as push abuse. Consider tying notifications to user-configured deal preferences and softening the "every hour / unlimited" marketing claims.
